beats.text: Here's a nesting-doll stack: biosphere, ecosystem, organism, organ, cell, molecule — each layer slides inside the next.
Zoom into one cell. A mitochondrion pulses, churning out ATP. That ATP powers everything above it — all the way up to a human eye catching light.

Now a timeline stretches across the screen. Earth forms at zero. Four billion years ago, the first cells flicker on. Then a long gap — one-point-five to two billion years more — before nucleated cells appear.
Same molecular machinery. Every creature, every kingdom, running the same core rulebook.
Master the shared grammar first. Every unique species becomes a dialect you can already read.
